delta_a.interpretation: POSITIVE — trained judge improves over baseline

delta_b.note: Per-task scores not available — bootstrap skipped
delta_b.interpretation: Prompting matches or beats training — honest finding, report in blog

cost_pareto.description: Per-task cost and latency with vs without trained judge component

baseline_heuristic.method: Pure Python regex + heuristic fallback, no GPU

trained_judge_gamma1_5.method: Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct + LoRA adapter inference on T4
trained_judge_gamma1_5.note: Cost is /bin/bash on free Colab T4; ~/bin/bash.0005 on RunPod 4090 at /bin/bash.34/hr

prompted_base_model.method: Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct base model inference on T4, no LoRA

cost_pareto.pareto_verdict: Prompted base model dominates on both quality (58% pass rate) and latency (1.54s vs 1.37s is negligible). Trained judge does not achieve a favorable cost-quality tradeoff at current data scale (599 pairs). Heuristic baseline is fastest (0.001s) but lowest quality (34%). Recommendation: use prompted base model as quality gate until trained judge is retrained at ~1000 pairs.
